message: docs: fix README install and CLI reality (bd-v56c)

 {"id":"bd-v2o7","title":"Reconcile stale README example-count snapshot","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:44:25.168927340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:26.141481114Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:26.141093820Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-v56c","title":"README: fix install/CLI claims after installer and cli target landed","description":"## Problem
README still says the tree does not include scripts/install.sh and does not build a standalone asx executable. That is no longer accurate:
- scripts/install.sh exists
- Makefile has a cli target that builds build/bin/asx
- src/cli/main.c implements version/info/doctor/help
- make install still installs only libasx.a + headers, so docs need a precise distinction between buildable CLI vs installed library surface

## What to do
1. Update README library-first wording so it matches the current tree.
2. Installation section: acknowledge scripts/install.sh exists, and keep package-manager claims scoped accurately.
3. Clarify that make cli builds build/bin/asx, while make install installs the library and headers only.
4. Keep the operator-surface wording precise: CLI exists as a convenience binary in-tree, not as the default installed artifact.

## Evidence
- scripts/install.sh exists
- Makefile: cli target at lines 529-532
- src/cli/main.c implements command handling
- README lines 58-59, 178-181, 371 contain stale claims

## Files
- README.md","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:28:29.076915515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:19.989435123Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:19.989174820Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-va8n","title":"EPIC: Local Lint and Format Tooling (GATE-CHECK)","description":"## Goal
Install and configure clang-format and clang-tidy locally so that make format-check and
make lint produce real results instead of SKIP.

## Why This Matters
GATE-CHECK (format + lint + strict build) is the first CI blocking gate. Currently these
targets skip locally because the tools are not installed, meaning developers and agents
cannot catch formatting or lint violations before pushing. This creates a feedback gap
where CI rejects commits that looked clean locally.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- clang-format (version matching CI) installed and on PATH
- clang-tidy (version matching CI) installed and on PATH
- make format-check runs and either passes or reports real violations
- make lint runs and either passes or reports real violations
- .clang-format and .clang-tidy configs match CI expectations

## Risk
- Low risk. This is purely toolchain installation.
- Version mismatch between local and CI clang-format could cause spurious diffs","notes":"## Dependency Structure
bd-va8n.1 (clang-format) and bd-va8n.2 (clang-tidy) are independent — both can
be done in parallel. No cross-epic dependencies; this is the lowest-priority
quality-of-life epic.

## CRITICAL: Config Files Do Not Exist
**Neither .clang-format nor .clang-tidy exist in this repository.** These beads
must CREATE the configuration files from scratch, deriving style rules from the
existing codebase patterns. This is more work than simply installing tools —
the style must be audited and codified.

## Rationale
The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION_20260312.md report (Gap #2) notes that format-check
and lint gates report SKIP locally. The gap is twofold: (1) the tools are not
installed, and (2) the config files do not exist.

## How This Serves Project Goals
This is developer/agent experience improvement, not a feature parity gap per se.
It's P3 because CI already enforces these checks — the gap is in local validation
speed, not correctness.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":105,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:36.760207363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.660348611Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.657555496Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dx","tooling"]}

@@ -54,9 +54,10 @@ make test
 | **Cross-profile semantic parity gates** | All profiles produce identical semantic digests for shared fixture sets |
 
 The current repository is library-first: it ships the static library, public
-headers, tests, examples, and release-packaging machinery. It does not
-currently include the `scripts/install.sh` installer shown in older docs, and
-it does not currently build or install a standalone `asx` executable.
+headers, tests, examples, release-packaging machinery, the in-tree
+`scripts/install.sh` helper, and a convenience CLI build target (`make cli`).
+`make install` still installs the library and headers only; the `asx` CLI is an
+in-tree convenience binary rather than the default installed artifact.
 
 ## Quick Repository Workflow
 
@@ -173,13 +174,14 @@ See `examples/` for more: channel flow, actor supervision, cancellation draining
 The current repository ships:
 - the static library `libasx.a`
 - public headers under `include/asx/`
+- the in-tree installer helper `scripts/install.sh`
+- the in-tree convenience CLI target `make cli` (builds `build/bin/asx`)
 - examples, tests, fixtures, and deterministic release packaging
 
 The current repository does not ship:
-- an in-tree `scripts/install.sh`
 - package-manager metadata that makes `brew install asx` / `apt install asx`
   / `opkg install asx` true for this repo by itself
-- a standalone `asx` executable
+- a default `make install` path that installs the `asx` CLI binary
 
 ### 1) From Source
 
@@ -197,6 +199,20 @@ make build-parallel  # compile-only deferred scaffold; not a Wave A parity/CI la
 `make install` currently installs the static library plus public headers under
 `$(PREFIX)`; it does not install a CLI binary.
 
+If you want the in-tree convenience CLI as well, build it explicitly:
+
+```bash
+make cli
+./build/bin/asx help
+```
+
+If you want a one-step source install helper for the library surface, the
+repository also ships:
+
+```bash
+./scripts/install.sh [--prefix=/usr/local]
+```
+
 ### 2) Release Artifacts
 
 ```bash
@@ -368,7 +384,7 @@ make release-artifacts RELEASE_VERSION=0.1.0 RELEASE_TARGET=linux-x86_64 PROFILE
 - Runtime construction: `include/asx/runtime/builder.h`, `include/asx/runtime/rt.h`, `include/asx/runtime/runtime.h`
 - App bootstrap helpers: `include/asx/app/app.h`
 - Doctor/reporting surfaces: `include/asx/app/doctor.h`, `include/asx/app/report.h`, `include/asx/console/console.h`
-- CLI embedding helpers (not a shipped executable): `include/asx/cli/cli.h`
+- CLI helpers and in-tree convenience binary surface: `include/asx/cli/cli.h`, `src/cli/main.c`, `make cli`
